checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox contract validates Relayer Agent cryptographic signatures
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Mailbox smart contract accurately verifies the cryptographic signatures on messages submitted by Relayer Agents to ensure message integrity and authenticity, preventing impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not implement strong authentication for Relayer Agents.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic signing of messages by authorized Relayer Agents and require Mailbox to verify these signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review, formal verification report of signature validation logic, unit and integration test reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement replay protection for Relayer Agent message processing
checklist_items.description: Ensure the Mailbox contract prevents replay attacks by implementing mechanisms (e.g., nonces, message hashes, block numbers) to invalidate previously processed or signed Relayer Agent messages and proofs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can forge or obtain valid Relayer credentials/signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic signing of messages by authorized Relayer Agents, along with replay protection mechanisms (e.g., nonces), and require Mailbox to verify these signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review focusing on nonce management or transaction uniqueness, security audit findings.

checklist_items.title: Securely manage and update authorized Relayer Agent identities
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Mailbox or an associated contract securely stores and manages the list of public keys or identities of authorized Relayer Agents, and that the update mechanism for this list is access-controlled and robust against unauthorized modification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not implement strong authentication for Relayer Agents.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic signing of messages by authorized Relayer Agents and require Mailbox to verify these signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review of access control for authorized key management, audit logs of key updates, deployment configuration review.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox cryptographically validates interchain message integrity
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Mailbox smart contract rigorously validates the cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., Merkle proofs, digital signatures) of all incoming interchain messages and associated proofs. This is crucial to prevent a compromised Relayer Agent from tampering with messages before submission to the destination Mailbox, ensuring the integrity of data processed on the target chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox's verification process does not detect all forms of message or proof tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust cryptographic integrity checks (e.g., Merkle proofs, digital signatures) for messages and proofs, verified by the Mailbox and ISMs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit report confirming cryptographic verification logic, or comprehensive test suite results demonstrating integrity validation across various tampering scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Verify Relayer economic incentives and disincentives
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the economic model for Relayer Agents includes sufficient incentives for consistent message delivery and disincentives (e.g., slashing) for malicious or negligent behavior to ensure protocol availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are insufficient incentives or penalties to ensure Relayer Agent availability and honest operation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement a decentralized network of Relayer Agents with economic incentives (e.g., fees) and disincentives (e.g., slashing) to ensure high availability and honest behavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of Relayer economic model, smart contract code for staking/slashing mechanisms, and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement Mailbox transaction rate-limiting and spam prevention
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Mailbox smart contract implements effective rate-limiting or spam-prevention mechanisms to prevent a malicious Relayer Agent from flooding it with excessive or invalid transactions, which would impact cross-chain communication availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox lacks effective rate-limiting or spam-prevention mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure high availability and honest behavior by preventing DoS attacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review results demonstrating rate-limiting logic, or unit/integration tests confirming prevention of transaction flooding.

checklist_items.title: Monitor Relayer Agent health and message relaying
checklist_items.description: Establish robust monitoring for Relayer Agents to detect failures in message delivery or abnormal transaction patterns (e.g., unusually low or high transaction volume from a relayer), enabling timely intervention to maintain service availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Relayer Agent is essential for message delivery. Its failure or malicious intent can directly halt cross-chain operations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement a decentralized network of Relayer Agents... to ensure high availability and honest behavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system dashboards, alert configurations, and incident response runbooks for relayer issues.

checklist_items.title: Verify secure logging of sensitive ABI-encoded data by CCIP-read Service Framework
checklist_items.description: Ensure the CCIP-read Service Framework does not log sensitive information from ABI-encoded request data in an insecure manner, preventing unauthorized information disclosure. This includes reviewing logging levels, data sanitization, and storage security practices.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CCIP-read Service Framework has vulnerabilities (e.g., insecure logging, unauthenticated access, software flaws).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, secure logging practices, and encryption for sensitive data processed by the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit of logging configurations, log samples, and code review findings showing sensitive data is not logged or is properly masked/encrypted.

checklist_items.title: Review access controls for the CCIP-read Service Framework API endpoint
checklist_items.description: Confirm that access to the CCIP-read Service Framework's API endpoint (receiving ABI-encoded data) is appropriately restricted and authenticated to prevent unauthorized access and subsequent information disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CCIP-read Service Framework has vulnerabilities (e.g., insecure logging, unauthenticated access, software flaws).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, secure logging practices, and encryption for sensitive data processed by the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files for API gateway/service, network access policies, authentication mechanism documentation, and penetration test results.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption of sensitive ABI-encoded data in transit and at rest within CCIP-read Service Framework
checklist_items.description: Check if sensitive information within ABI-encoded request data is encrypted both during transmission to the CCIP-read Service Framework and when processed or stored temporarily by the framework to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The ABI-encoded request data contains sensitive information that should remain confidential.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, secure logging practices, and encryption for sensitive data processed by the CCIP-read Service Framework.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network traffic capture analysis, database/file system encryption configuration, and code review confirming encryption library usage for sensitive data.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox contract enforces strong cryptographic authentication for Validator Agent submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure the Mailbox smart contract rigorously validates digital signatures or other cryptographic proofs from Validator Agents for all message verifications and checkpoint submissions, preventing unauthorized actors from impersonating legitimate Validator Agents and compromising message integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox does not sufficiently authenticate the source of verification data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong cryptographic authentication for Validator Agent submissions (e.g., digital signatures).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Detailed audit report of Mailbox smart contract code verifying the correct implementation and enforcement of cryptographic signature validation for all relevant entry points for Validator Agent submissions.

checklist_items.title: Verify end-to-end integrity checks for interchain messages
checklist_items.description: Confirm that cryptographic hashes (e.g., Merkle proofs) are correctly implemented and utilized to ensure the integrity of interchain messages and checkpoints during transit and storage, prior to their verification by the Validator Agent. This includes checks performed at message origination, transit, and reception points.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Messages or checkpoints are not sufficiently protected against modification during transit or storage before verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement end-to-end integrity checks (e.g., cryptographic hashes, Merkle proofs) for messages and checkpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report confirming cryptographic integrity check implementation, and/or automated test results demonstrating integrity validation.

checklist_items.title: Assess Validator Agent's tamper detection robustness
checklist_items.description: Conduct a thorough review of the Validator Agent's verification logic and mechanisms to ensure its ability to reliably detect various forms of tampering, including data modification, replay attacks, or reordering of interchain messages and checkpoints. This includes validating its interaction with the Mailbox contract.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's verification logic is unable to detect all forms of tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement end-to-end integrity checks (e.g., cryptographic hashes, Merkle proofs) for messages and checkpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit report, static/dynamic analysis findings, or penetration test results specifically targeting Validator Agent's verification capabilities.

checklist_items.title: Verify cryptographic signing of Validator Agent submissions
checklist_items.description: Confirm that all Validator Agent message verification and checkpoint submissions to the Mailbox smart contract are cryptographically signed to ensure the agent cannot repudiate its actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not record irrefutable proof of a Validator Agent's verification actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Validator Agent verification submissions are cryptographically signed and logged on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review confirming signature verification logic for Validator Agent submissions. Automated test results validating signature enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Ensure on-chain logging of Validator Agent verification actions
checklist_items.description: Check that critical Validator Agent actions, specifically message verifications and checkpoint submissions, are immutably logged on-chain with sufficient detail (e.g., signer, timestamp, action specifics) to establish non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not record irrefutable proof of a Validator Agent's verification actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Validator Agent verification submissions are cryptographically signed and logged on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract event logs or state variables demonstrating the persistent and immutable storage of Validator Agent submission details on-chain. Blockchain explorer query results confirming logged data.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption of sensitive interchain message payloads
checklist_items.description: Confirm that sensitive data contained within interchain messages or checkpoints, where confidentiality is required, is properly encrypted before transmission and protected during processing by the Mailbox and Validator Agent.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Interchain messages or checkpoints contain sensitive information not intended for public exposure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement encryption for sensitive message payloads where confidentiality is required.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of encryption scheme, relevant code review findings, or audit logs confirming encryption during data flow.

checklist_items.title: Ensure secure operational environment for Validator Agent
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Validator Agent's operational environment (e.g., host systems, network access, runtime environment) is secured against unauthorized access to prevent disclosure of sensitive information.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's operational environment or logging is not adequately secured against unauthorized access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure Validator Agent's operational environment and logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security hardening guidelines, configuration audits, access control policies, and penetration test results for the Validator Agent's infrastructure.

checklist_items.title: Prevent sensitive data leakage through Validator Agent logs
checklist_items.description: Review Validator Agent logging configurations and practices to ensure that sensitive interchain message data or checkpoint information is not inadvertently logged or is properly redacted/encrypted within logs to prevent information disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's operational environment or logging is not adequately secured against unauthorized access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure Validator Agent's operational environment and logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logging policy documents, log redaction/encryption configurations, and sample log audits.

checklist_items.title: Verify rate limiting for Validator Agent interactions with Mailbox
checklist_items.description: Check if the Validator Agent's network interactions with the Mailbox (data flow 20) are protected by sufficient rate limiting mechanisms to prevent resource exhaustion from excessive queries. This includes inbound requests to the agent and outbound queries from the agent if applicable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no sufficient rate limits or anti-DoS mechanisms in place for the Validator Agent's interactions with the Mailbox.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, robust infrastructure scaling, and monitoring for Validator Agents to withstand DoS attacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files demonstrating rate limit settings (e.g., API gateway, network ACLs), or penetration test results verifying rate limit effectiveness.

checklist_items.title: Assess Validator Agent infrastructure resilience and scaling
checklist_items.description: Evaluate the Validator Agent's underlying infrastructure (e.g., compute, network, storage) for its ability to withstand and recover from resource exhaustion attacks (e.g., network floods, CPU spikes). Ensure proper auto-scaling, load balancing, and redundancy are implemented.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's infrastructure is vulnerable to resource exhaustion attacks (e.g., network floods, excessive queries).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, robust infrastructure scaling, and monitoring for Validator Agents to withstand DoS attacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Architecture diagrams, cloud provider infrastructure configurations (e.g., ASG, LB setup), stress test reports, or disaster recovery documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify DoS monitoring and alerting for Validator Agents
checklist_items.description: Ensure comprehensive monitoring solutions are deployed to detect anomalies related to Denial of Service attacks targeting Validator Agents, such as unusual traffic spikes, high resource utilization, or service unavailability. Verify that appropriate alerts are configured to notify responsible teams.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator Agent's infrastructure is vulnerable to resource exhaustion attacks (e.g., network floods, excessive queries).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, robust infrastructure scaling, and monitoring for Validator Agents to withstand DoS attacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards, alert configurations (thresholds, notification channels), and logs showing alert triggers and responses.

checklist_items.title: Verify multi-agent consensus for message verification
checklist_items.description: Check that the Mailbox smart contract enforces a robust consensus mechanism (e.g., multi-signature or threshold signature scheme) requiring attestations from multiple independent Validator Agents before accepting message verifications or checkpoints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox or ISM relies solely on the Validator Agent's attestation without independent verification or sufficient redundancy.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement a robust consensus mechanism among multiple independent Validator Agents (e.g., multi-signature, fraud proofs) and strict slashing conditions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review, audit report, or test results demonstrating enforcement of multi-signature or N-of-M consensus for message verification.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict slashing conditions for misbehaving validators
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the protocol's economic security model includes strict slashing conditions for Validator Agents that submit fraudulent, incorrect, or malicious verification results, ensuring an economic disincentive for privilege elevation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A single Validator Agent or a small collusion of agents can influence the acceptance of invalid messages.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement a robust consensus mechanism among multiple independent Validator Agents (e.g., multi-signature, fraud proofs) and strict slashing conditions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review of slashing logic, economic model documentation, and test cases covering various slashing scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Evaluate fraud proof mechanisms for message verification
checklist_items.description: Assess if the system incorporates effective fraud proof mechanisms that allow any honest participant to challenge and definitively prove the invalidity of a fraudulent message verification submitted by a Validator Agent, leading to its rejection and potential slashing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox or ISM relies solely on the Validator Agent's attestation without independent verification or sufficient redundancy.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement a robust consensus mechanism among multiple independent Validator Agents (e.g., multi-signature, fraud proofs) and strict slashing conditions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review of fraud proof implementation, design documentation, and dedicated test cases for fraud proof scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Verify Multi-Factor Authentication for Deployer Account
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Deployer Account utilizes strong multi-factor authentication (MFA) to prevent unauthorized access and spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system relies solely on single-factor authentication for the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong multi-factor authentication (MFA) and hardware security modules (HSM) for the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation or audit logs demonstrating MFA enforcement for the Deployer Account.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Deployer Account Private Keys are Protected by HSMs
checklist_items.description: Verify that the private keys or cryptographic material used by the Deployer Account are stored and managed within Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) to enhance protection against compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The attacker can obtain or bypass the authentication credentials of the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong multi-factor authentication (MFA) and hardware security modules (HSM) for the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System architecture diagrams, vendor attestations, or audit reports confirming HSM integration and usage for Deployer Account keys.

checklist_items.title: Verify multi-party signing for critical Interchain Account deployments
checklist_items.description: Confirm that critical Interchain Account deployment transactions are subject to multi-party signing (e.g., multi-sig wallet, governance vote) to prevent unauthorized or malicious single-point tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can intercept or modify the deployment transaction or configuration before it is submitted by the Deployer Account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party signing for critical deployments and cryptographic hashing of deployment configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of multi-sig policy, transaction logs showing multiple signatures for deployments, or governance vote results.

checklist_items.title: Implement and verify cryptographic integrity checks for deployment parameters
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Interchain Account deployment configurations and bytecode are cryptographically hashed, and that these hashes are validated on-chain or off-chain before actual deployment by the Deployer Account to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not sufficiently verify the integrity of the deployment parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party signing for critical deployments and cryptographic hashing of deployment configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review demonstrating hashing and validation logic, automated test results confirming integrity checks pass, or audit logs of successful hash validations.

checklist_items.title: Verify immutable and verifiable deployment records
checklist_items.description: Check that all interchain account deployment actions performed by the Deployer Account are comprehensively logged in an immutable and verifiable manner, ensuring non-repudiation and traceability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not maintain immutable, verifiable records of all deployments and their initiators.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging and auditing for all deployment actions, including transaction hashes and timestamps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report or blockchain explorer verification demonstrating immutable transaction records for all deployments by the Deployer Account.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Deployer Account actions are cryptographically signed and timestamped
checklist_items.description: Confirm that every deployment action initiated by the Deployer Account is cryptographically signed and includes an auditable timestamp within the transaction data or blockchain itself, enabling irrefutable proof of origin and action time.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Deployer Account's actions are not cryptographically signed and timestamped in an auditable manner.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging and auditing for all deployment actions, including transaction hashes and timestamps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction receipt showing a valid cryptographic signature and timestamp from the Deployer Account for a deployment.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption of Deployer Account sensitive data at rest
checklist_items.description: Ensure that private keys, deployment configurations, and other sensitive parameters associated with the Deployer Account are encrypted when stored on disk or in databases.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Sensitive data (e.g., private keys, deployment secrets) related to the Deployer Account is stored or transmitted insecurely.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit, and implement strict access controls based on least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of storage encryption policies, configuration screenshots, or audit reports confirming data encryption at rest.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption of Deployer Account sensitive data in transit
checklist_items.description: Confirm that all communication channels used for transmitting sensitive data (private keys, deployment configurations) to and from the Deployer Account are secured with strong encryption protocols (e.g., TLS 1.2+).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Sensitive data (e.g., private keys, deployment secrets) related to the Deployer Account is stored or transmitted insecurely.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit, and implement strict access controls based on least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network architecture diagrams, security group rules, or penetration test results verifying secure communication protocols.

checklist_items.title: Audit access controls for Deployer Account and associated sensitive data
checklist_items.description: Review and verify that strict access controls, based on the principle of least privilege, are applied to the Deployer Account itself and all environments/systems holding its private keys or deployment configurations. Ensure only authorized personnel and automated processes have access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Access controls to the environment where the Deployer Account operates are weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit, and implement strict access controls based on least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: IAM policies, access control matrix, audit logs of access attempts, or results from access control reviews.

checklist_items.title: Verify robust RPC provider redundancy for deployment
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the deployment system utilizes multiple, independent RPC providers, including failover mechanisms, to ensure Interchain Account deployment can proceed even if one provider fails or is attacked.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The deployment process does not have built-in retry mechanisms or alternative RPC providers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust RPC redundancy
checklist_items.evidence_required: Architectural documentation, configuration files, and network diagrams illustrating multi-RPC setup and failover logic.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Deployer Account maintains adequate operational funds
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Deployer Account's wallet consistently holds sufficient gas and operational funds to facilitate all necessary Interchain Account deployment transactions, preventing disruption due to resource exhaustion or fee spikes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can flood RPC endpoints or deplete the Deployer Account's operational funds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ensure sufficient operational funds
checklist_items.evidence_required: Treasury management policies, periodic balance reports, and historical transaction logs for the Deployer Account.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive network and transaction monitoring for deployments
checklist_items.description: Establish continuous monitoring for network latency, transaction failures, and RPC provider health during Interchain Account deployment operations, with automated alerting for anomalies indicating potential DoS attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can flood RPC endpoints or deplete the Deployer Account's operational funds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: monitor network health for deployment operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system configurations, dashboard views, and alert logs demonstrating proactive detection capabilities.

checklist_items.title: Verify robust retry mechanisms in deployment process
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Interchain Account deployment client or service incorporates robust retry logic and intelligent RPC provider selection/failover, ensuring resilience against transient network issues or targeted RPC attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The deployment process does not have built-in retry mechanisms or alternative RPC providers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust RPC redundancy
checklist_items.evidence_required: Source code review of deployment scripts/client, integration test results demonstrating failover, and deployment workflow documentation.

checklist_items.title: Restrict Deployer Account privileges to only deployment functions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Deployer Account's permissions are strictly limited to deploying Interchain Accounts and do not include capabilities to modify unrelated critical system parameters or execute unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Deployer Account has permissions to modify critical system parameters unrelated to ICA deployment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict least privilege principles for the Deployer Account and all deployed Interchain Accounts, limiting their capabilities to only what is necessary.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report of smart contract access control configurations governing the Deployer Account and review of deployment scripts.

checklist_items.title: Ensure deployed Interchain Accounts adhere to least privilege
checklist_items.description: Confirm that all Interchain Accounts deployed by the Deployer Account are configured with the minimum necessary permissions for their intended function, preventing inherited or configured excessive privileges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Deployed Interchain Accounts are not configured with the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict least privilege principles for the Deployer Account and all deployed Interchain Accounts, limiting their capabilities to only what is necessary.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Review of Interchain Account smart contract code and configuration parameters for deployed instances to verify restricted permissions.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication is enforced for Scraper Agent to Postgres Database connection
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Scraper Agent-Postgres Database connection enforces strong authentication mechanisms, such as mutual TLS (mTLS) or secure API keys, to prevent an attacker from impersonating the Scraper Agent and sending fraudulent database commands or data.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Scraper Agent's connection to the Postgres Database lacks strong mutual authentication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication mechanisms (e.g., mTLS, API keys) for the Scraper Agent's connection to the database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files for Scraper Agent and Postgres Database, network traffic analysis confirming mTLS handshake or API key validation, and audit logs of successful authenticated connections.

checklist_items.title: Verify data integrity protection for Scraper-to-DB communication
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the communication channel between the Scraper Agent and the Postgres Database (ID 21) uses mechanisms (e.g., TLS, secure protocols) to protect the integrity of 'Database commands, indexed data' (Data Flow ID 12) against unauthorized modification during transmission.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The communication channel between the Scraper Agent and the database is not integrity-protected (e.g., no encryption or hashing).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement data integrity checks and encryption for data in transit and at rest.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration review of Scraper Agent and Postgres Database connection settings; network traffic capture analysis to confirm use of secure protocols.

checklist_items.title: Verify strict access controls on Postgres Database
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Postgres Database (ID 21) storing 'Indexed Blockchain Data' (Asset ID 107) has strict authentication and authorization controls in place to prevent unauthorized direct data manipulation or access. This includes strong password policies, least privilege principles, and role-based access controls for all users and services.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database lacks sufficient access controls or is vulnerable to direct data manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict access controls on the database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database access control configuration review; audit logs of database access attempts; vulnerability scan results related to database access.

checklist_items.title: Verify encryption for Indexed Blockchain Data at rest
checklist_items.description: Confirm that 'Indexed Blockchain Data' (Asset ID 107) stored within the Postgres Database (ID 21) is encrypted at rest to protect its integrity against direct unauthorized access or manipulation (e.g., if underlying storage is compromised).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database lacks sufficient access controls or is vulnerable to direct data manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement data integrity checks and encryption for data in transit and at rest.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Postgres Database encryption configuration review; underlying storage encryption validation; data storage mechanism review.

checklist_items.title: Verify comprehensive and immutable logging for Scraper Agent database operations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all database operations performed by the Scraper Agent on the Postgres Database are logged comprehensively, and these logs are immutable to prevent alteration or deletion, thereby supporting non-repudiation and accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Database logging of Scraper Agent actions is insufficient or can be easily altered.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging for all database operations and consider cryptographic signing of critical data updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation for database logging, audit logs showing immutability setup (e.g., WORM storage policies), and sample log entries demonstrating comprehensive capture of Scraper Agent actions.

checklist_items.title: Evaluate and implement cryptographic signing for critical Scraper Agent database writes
checklist_items.description: Assess the feasibility and implement cryptographic signing for critical data updates performed by the Scraper Agent on the Postgres Database. This provides verifiable proofs of data origin and action execution, preventing the Scraper Agent from repudiating its writes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: No cryptographic signatures or verifiable proofs are generated for database operations performed by the Scraper Agent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive, immutable logging for all database operations and consider cryptographic signing of critical data updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review results showing cryptographic signing logic implementation, design documentation outlining which database operations are signed, and examples of signed data entries within the database.

checklist_items.title: Verify network firewall rules restrict access to Postgres Database
checklist_items.description: Check that network firewalls (e.g., security groups, network ACLs) are configured to only allow necessary and authorized IP addresses or services to connect to the Postgres Database, preventing unauthorized network access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database is not adequately protected by network firewalls or access controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict network access controls
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot of network firewall rules or configuration, audit logs of network changes.

checklist_items.title: Confirm indexed blockchain data is encrypted at rest in Postgres
checklist_items.description: Verify that all indexed blockchain data stored in the Postgres Database is encrypted at rest using industry-standard encryption mechanisms (e.g., disk encryption, TDE), protecting against disclosure if the underlying storage is compromised.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Data stored in the database is not encrypted at rest.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: encrypt data at rest
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database configuration settings confirming encryption, cloud provider console screenshots.

checklist_items.title: Validate strong authentication is enforced for Postgres Database access
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all accounts used to access the Postgres Database (e.g., by the Scraper agent or administrators) utilize strong, unique credentials, multi-factor authentication where applicable, or integrated identity management solutions (e.g., IAM roles), and that default or weak credentials are disabled.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak or default credentials are used for database access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: implement strong authentication for database access.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Database user/role configuration, authentication policy documentation, audit logs of authentication attempts.

checklist_items.title: Verify Postgres Database connection rate limiting
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Postgres Database is configured with appropriate rate limiting mechanisms to prevent connection floods or excessive requests.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database lacks sufficient resource limits or rate limiting to withstand an attack.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting... for the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files showing rate limit settings, or test results demonstrating rate limiting in action.

checklist_items.title: Validate network access controls for Postgres Database
checklist_items.description: Ensure that network firewalls and security groups are configured to restrict access to the Postgres Database only from authorized IP ranges and necessary ports.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The network infrastructure protecting the database is vulnerable to DoS attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement... network filtering... for the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network security group rules, firewall configurations, or network access control lists.

checklist_items.title: Assess Postgres Database redundancy and failover
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Postgres Database deployment includes redundancy (e.g., replication, clustering) and automated failover mechanisms to ensure high availability and resilience against single points of failure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The network infrastructure protecting the database is vulnerable to DoS attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: ensure robust infrastructure with redundancy for the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Architecture diagrams, replication setup details, failover test reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement and monitor DoS detection for Postgres Database
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Postgres Database and its surrounding infrastructure have logging and monitoring systems in place to detect and alert on potential Denial of Service attack patterns, such as unusual traffic spikes or failed connection attempts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database lacks sufficient resource limits or rate limiting to withstand an attack.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, network filtering, and ensure robust infrastructure with redundancy for the Postgres Database.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboard screenshots, alert configurations, incident response playbooks for DoS.

checklist_items.title: Verify Scraper Agent operates with least necessary privileges
checklist_items.description: Ensure the Scraper Agent's operating system user or service account has only the minimum required permissions to connect to, write, and read from the Postgres Database, and no excessive system-level privileges that could allow privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Scraper Agent runs with more privileges than strictly necessary for its operations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply the principle of least privilege to the Scraper Agent.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration review report detailing Scraper Agent's user/service account permissions and database access controls.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Postgres Database and host system are regularly patched
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Postgres Database server and its underlying operating system (OS) are kept up-to-date with the latest security patches and updates to address known vulnerabilities that could lead to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Postgres Database or its underlying operating system has unpatched vulnerabilities that allow privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly patch and update the Postgres Database and its host system.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Patch management logs, vulnerability scan reports showing no critical/high vulnerabilities related to the database or OS.

checklist_items.title: Verify TokenRouter's robust signature validation for remote transfers
checklist_items.description: Thoroughly review and test the TokenRouter contract's implementation to ensure all incoming remote token transfer requests are authenticated via robust cryptographic signature verification. This includes validating correct signing keys, ensuring message integrity, and implementing protection against replay attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TokenRouter does not sufficiently verify the authenticity of the transfer initiator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong multi-factor authentication for user wallets and robust signature verification for transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit report detailing signature validation logic, automated test coverage report for the `transferRemote` function's authentication, or code review findings of signature verification implementation.

checklist_items.title: Verify Mailbox's on-chain message integrity proof verification
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Mailbox smart contract rigorously validates all cryptographic proofs and message content submitted by Relayer agents. This validation must ensure that the message, its origin, and its parameters (e.g., recipient, amount) have not been tampered with during transit, preventing malicious alterations by a compromised Relayer.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Mailbox's proof verification mechanism is insufficient to detect message tampering by the Relayer.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust cryptographic proofs and on-chain verification mechanisms for all relayed messages.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit report confirming the robustness of proof verification logic, accompanied by comprehensive unit and integration test results demonstrating resistance to message tampering.

checklist_items.title: Verify On-chain Logging of Relayer Actions within Mailbox
checklist_items.description: Check if the Mailbox smart contract correctly implements comprehensive on-chain logging for all critical actions performed by Relayer agents, such as message processing and delivery. This includes recording sufficient details like transaction hashes, sender addresses, message identifiers, and timestamps to ensure auditable records and non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is no robust on-chain logging or incentive/penalty mechanism for Relayer actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement clear on-chain logging of Relayer actions and a robust incentive/penalty mechanism for message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review demonstrating explicit event emissions for relayer actions; successful transaction logs showing emitted events for processed messages.

checklist_items.title: Assess Relayer Incentive and Penalty Mechanism in Mailbox
checklist_items.description: Evaluate the Mailbox smart contract's design and configuration to confirm the presence and effectiveness of a robust economic incentive or penalty mechanism. This mechanism should actively motivate Relayer agents to deliver messages reliably and deter repudiation or failure to process messages, ensuring accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is no robust on-chain logging or incentive/penalty mechanism for Relayer actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement clear on-chain logging of Relayer actions and a robust incentive/penalty mechanism for message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review confirming implementation of economic incentives/penalties; design documentation outlining the mechanism's logic and parameters; audit reports on the mechanism's robustness and economic viability.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong access controls for Github Proxy
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Github Proxy enforces least privilege access controls, limiting who can access or modify its configuration and data, especially sensitive API keys, to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy is vulnerable to attack or misconfiguration. Sensitive API keys or configuration data are stored or transmitted via the Github Proxy.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong access controls... for the Github Proxy and sensitive data it handles.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policies, IAM configurations, or audit logs demonstrating restricted access to Github Proxy resources and configurations.

checklist_items.title: Ensure sensitive data handled by Github Proxy is encrypted
checklist_items.description: Verify that all sensitive protocol configuration data and API keys stored or transmitted via the Github Proxy are encrypted both in transit (e.g., using TLS) and at rest to prevent information disclosure if the proxy is compromised.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Sensitive API keys or configuration data are stored or transmitted via the Github Proxy.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement... encryption... for the Github Proxy and sensitive data it handles.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings showing encryption protocols (e.g., TLS, AES-256), data storage encryption configurations, or network traffic analysis.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits of Github Proxy
checklist_items.description: Establish and perform a schedule for regular security audits, vulnerability assessments, and penetration tests on the Github Proxy, its configurations, and the sensitive data it handles to identify and remediate potential information disclosure vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy is vulnerable to attack or misconfiguration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement... regular security audits for the Github Proxy and sensitive data it handles.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, vulnerability scan results, or a documented audit schedule with completed audit records.

checklist_items.title: Verify Post-Dispatch Hook handles insufficient payment gracefully
checklist_items.description: Check the 'postDispatch' function in the Post-Dispatch Hook (ID: 12) to ensure it handles insufficient payment without unconditionally reverting the transaction, allowing for graceful processing, logging, or partial execution to prevent denial of service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The 'postDispatch' function of a hook is configured to revert on insufficient payment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust fee quoting mechanisms and ensure sufficient payment is enforced or handled gracefully (e.g., partial processing, refunding excess).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings or audit report confirming the graceful handling of insufficient payments.

checklist_items.title: Validate accuracy and robustness of fee quoting mechanism for Post-Dispatch Hooks
checklist_items.description: Evaluate the mechanism responsible for quoting fees for Post-Dispatch Hooks (ID: 12) to ensure it accurately calculates the required payment, reducing the likelihood of users submitting transactions with insufficient value that could trigger reverts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can submit transactions with insufficient value for hook execution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust fee quoting mechanisms and ensure sufficient payment is enforced or handled gracefully (e.g., partial processing, refunding excess).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation, test reports, or audit findings on the fee quoting system's accuracy and robustness.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring for Post-Dispatch Hook transaction failures
checklist_items.description: Deploy automated monitoring and alerting for transactions interacting with Post-Dispatch Hooks (ID: 12), specifically tracking reverts or failures of the 'postDispatch' function, to detect potential denial of service attempts related to insufficient payment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can submit transactions with insufficient value for hook execution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust fee quoting mechanisms and ensure sufficient payment is enforced or handled gracefully (e.g., partial processing, refunding excess).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot of monitoring dashboard, alert configurations, and runbook for incident response.

checklist_items.title: Verify deployment of multiple independent Relayer agents.
checklist_items.description: Check that the system is configured and operating with multiple independent Relayer agents to prevent single points of failure for message delivery.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no sufficient redundancy or incentive mechanisms to ensure continuous message relaying.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multiple independent Relayer agents and a robust incentive/penalty system to ensure message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Architectural diagrams, deployment configurations, and operational logs demonstrating the presence and activity of multiple independent relayer instances.

checklist_items.title: Review Relayer incentive and penalty mechanisms for robustness.
checklist_items.description: Evaluate the design and implementation of the Relayer incentive and penalty system to ensure it effectively motivates continuous message relaying and deters malicious or negligent behavior. This includes reviewing contract logic and off-chain policy.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no sufficient redundancy or incentive mechanisms to ensure continuous message relaying.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multiple independent Relayer agents and a robust incentive/penalty system to ensure message delivery.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review reports for incentive mechanisms, documentation of off-chain penalty policies, and analysis of economic models.

checklist_items.title: Verify ISM implements robust replay protection
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Interchain Security Module (ISM) incorporates effective replay protection mechanisms, such as nonces, unique message IDs, or time-based validity, to prevent unauthorized re-execution of previously processed interchain messages and safeguard against privilege escalation or state manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A deployed ISM does not implement sufficient replay protection.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce replay protection mechanisms (e.g., nonces, unique message IDs) within all Interchain Security Modules.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review findings, audit report detailing replay protection implementation, or automated test results demonstrating replay attack resistance.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls for deployment keys and environments
checklist_items.description: Verify that deployment private keys are protected by strict access controls and that the Hyperlane CLI/SDK deployment environment is hardened against compromise, limiting access to authorized personnel only.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Hyperlane CLI/SDK environment is compromised, allowing an attacker to execute deployment commands.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls for deployment keys
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control lists (ACLs) for key storage, environment hardening configurations, and documented access policies.

checklist_items.title: Require hardware security modules (HSMs) for all deployment keys
checklist_items.description: Confirm that private keys used for deploying critical Hyperlane smart contracts and modifying protocol configurations are exclusively stored and used within FIPS 140-2 compliant hardware security modules (HSMs).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A developer's private key used for deployments is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: use hardware security modules (HSMs)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of HSM integration, audit logs showing key usage from HSMs, and hardware attestation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce multi-signature for critical smart contract deployments and configuration updates
checklist_items.description: Verify that the deployment process for core Hyperlane smart contracts (e.g., Mailbox) and any subsequent critical protocol configuration changes on-chain (e.g., Protocol Configuration Data) are secured by multi-signature mechanisms requiring approval from multiple authorized parties.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A developer's private key used for deployments is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: enforce multi-signature requirements for critical deployments.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction records demonstrating multi-signature approvals for deployments/updates, and smart contract code review confirming multi-sig governance.
